venue_label: Australia Pavilion (Denton Corker Marshall, 2015 — only 21st-century Giardini pavilion)

private_funders.name: Simon Mordant AO
private_funders.notes: Global Ambassador and Advocate; previously co-led the AUD 7.5m construction of the current pavilion (AUD 1m public, AUD 6.5m from 82 private donors).

reception_summary: 'conference of one's self' is an immersive multisensory installation centred on eight 3x2m canvases arranged in an octagon. Sabsabi simultaneously appears in main exhibition 'In Minor Keys' at the Arsenale.
red_flags: MAJOR Sabsabi controversy 2025: Creative Australia announced and then rescinded the commission within a week after a column in The Australian and Senate questioning targeted earlier works; reinstated July 2025 after Blackhall & Pearl independent governance review found governance failures (no political interference).

curator_affiliation: Gropius Bau, Berlin
commissioning_body: BMWKMS (Federal Ministry for Arts, Culture, Media and Sport)
selection_method_notes: Open call with 54 submissions; three-stage selection by expert jury (Spindler, Hellberg, Krejci, Nori, Schinwald); federal minister makes final nomination.
public_funding_sources: Lower Austria, Salzburg, Carinthia state governments

private_funders.name: Galerie Thaddaeus Ropac
private_funders.notes: Holzinger's commercial gallery is co-funding her state representation — status/cash exchange made literal.

private_funders.name: Laufen
private_funders.notes: Sponsor whose product is thematically embedded in the closed-loop sewage tank artwork.

reception_summary: Finestre sull'Arte: 'the most talked-about pavilion at the 2026 Biennale'. Two-hour-plus queues. Bell tolled by nude performer; jet ski in flooded interior; sewage tank fed by visitors' Biennale toilet waste.
red_flags: Artist's own dealer (Ropac) co-funding her state pavilion.

private_funders.name: Proximus Art Collection

private_funders.name: Friends of the Belgian Pavilion 2026
private_funders.sector: patron circle (Art Brussels, Art Antwerp, KU Leuven, National Bank of Belgium, Zeno X Gallery, etc.)

reception_summary: Cultured: Warlop brings rock-and-roll energy to a typically staid Giardini pavilion. First time performance art is the central medium of the Belgian Pavilion.
red_flags: CONFIRMED closure on 8 May 2026 in solidarity with ANGA strike. Entrance blocked with white plaster tiles spelling 'STOP'. Artist statement: 'We refuse to share this platform with a genocidal state.' Commissioner Caroline Gennez publicly endorsed the closure.

Political opening by Vučić-circle figures.

venue_label: Arsenale (relocated from Giardini pavilion, which is closed for renovation)
pavilion_owner: State of Israel (permanent Giardini pavilion under renovation; 2026 in Arsenale)
artist_born: 1959, Bucharest (immigrated to Israel 1973); 2025 Israel Prize laureate
curator_affiliation: Independent (no institutional affiliation disclosed)
commissioning_body: Israel Ministry of Culture and Sport jointly with Ministry of Foreign Affairs; commissioner Michael Gov
selection_method_notes: Culture Ministry call substantially delayed (Haaretz, April 2025); selection ultimately proceeded under tight timelines through ministerial process rather than fully open competition.
public_funding_sources: Israel Ministry of Culture and Sport
reception_summary: Most politically charged pavilion of 2026. Hundreds of pro-Palestine activists physically blocked the entrance on 6 May. Did NOT close in solidarity on 8 May; remained behind heavy security cordon (or shuttered with armed police, per conflicting reports).
red_flags: Five-person Biennale jury resigned ~30 April after Fainaru issued legal warnings to the Biennale, Italian Ministry of Culture and PM's office, citing 'antisemitism and nationality-based discrimination' — Golden Lion abolished, replaced with Visitors' Lions.
ANGA-led 24-hour strike on 8 May (first in Biennale history) had Israel pavilion removal as central demand; ~27 pavilions partially or fully closed in solidarity.
Israeli MFA publicly attacked the jury as 'a contamination of the art world'.
Italian Culture Minister Giuli phoned Fainaru and reportedly pledged ministry-level promotion.
L'Internationale criticised the Arsenale placement as 'an explicit institutional endorsement'.

curator_affiliation: Independent curator
commissioning_body: Angelo Piero Cappello, General Directorate for Contemporary Creativity, Italian Ministry of Culture
selection_method_notes: Direct appointment by the Ministry of Culture's General Directorate for Contemporary Creativity.
reception_summary: Largely overshadowed by strike, jury collapse, and Russia/Israel controversies in opening week.

Subject matter (heritage destruction in Gaza, Lebanon, Syria, Iraq) lets the state align with pro-Palestinian sympathy while sidestepping any Saudi foreign policy criticism (e.g. Yemen).

pavilion_owner: Rented from Scuola Internazionale di Grafica
artist_name: Agus Suwage, Syahrizal Pahlevi, R.E. Hartanto, Theresia Agustina Sitompul, Mariam Sofrina, others
curator_affiliation: Curator, National Gallery of Indonesia; lecturer ITB
commissioning_body: Ministry of Culture, Republic of Indonesia (Endah T.D. Retnoastuti)
selection_method_notes: Curator appointed by Minister; assembled artist line-up. Ends six-year hiatus.
reception_summary: Welcomed as substantive return; concept built on a 15th-century Sumatran imaginary voyage to Venice.
red_flags: Use of new sovereign-linked Danantara fund for cultural diplomacy may invite scrutiny.

pavilion_name: Pavilion of the Republic of Kazakhstan
artist_name: Group of 9: Mukanova, Tattibayeva, Ligay (ADYR-ASPAN), Aubakir, Kadyrkhanova, Bayaliyev, Nurakhmet, Smagambetov, Kaboke
artist_born: All Kazakhstani; spans late-Soviet generation to mid-career
curator_affiliation: Independent Kazakh artist-curator
commissioning_body: Ministry of Culture and Information of Kazakhstan (Aida Balayeva, Deputy PM)
selection_method_notes: First time a Central Asian country selected its pavilion through a public competitive process.
public_funding_sources: Kazakhstan Ministry of Culture and Information
reception_summary: ArtAsiaPacific framed it as institutional milestone for Central Asia.

pavilion_name: Pavilion of the Republic of Kosovo
venue_label: Chiesa di Santa Maria del Pianto, Castello (newly reactivated 17th-century church)
curator_affiliation: Executive Director and Chief Curator, Storefront for Art and Architecture (NY)
commissioning_body: National Gallery of Kosovo (commissioner: Hana Halilaj)
selection_method_notes: Standard Kosovo process: National Gallery juries proposals.
public_funding_sources: Kosovo Ministry of Culture, Youth and Sport
reception_summary: Strongly positive; Artforum '1,000 Words' feature. Reactivated long-closed historic church.
red_flags: Pavilion leans heavily on private collectors and one of the artist's own commercial galleries (ERMES ERMES).

venue_label: Ex Chiesa di Santa Caterina, Convitto Foscarini, Cannaregio
curator_affiliation: Italian art historian, independent curator
commissioning_body: Embassy of the Kyrgyz Republic in Italy
selection_method_notes: First-ever Kyrgyz national pavilion; no open call.
public_funding_sources: Embassy/Ministry channels of the Kyrgyz Republic
reception_summary: ArtAsiaPacific, Canvas, AKIpress treat it as a debut milestone.
red_flags: Artist is a full member of the Russian Academy of Arts (since 2013) and was director of the Moscow State Academic Art School — representing Kyrgyzstan with a Russian-institutional artist in the same Biennale that has readmitted Russia is politically loaded.

curator_affiliation: Independent; founding director of Block Universe
commissioning_body: Lithuanian National Museum of Art (commissioner: Lolita Jablonskienė)
selection_method_notes: Closed/curatorial selection by national museum commissioner.
reception_summary: Three-channel film installation building on Warmblooded and Earthbound (2024). Lithuania, Latvia, Estonia jointly staged a pro-Ukraine procession at opening.
red_flags: KANAL co-production blurs national-versus-Belgian funding lines.

venue_label: Spazio Santa Veneranda, SS. Geremia e Lucia, Cannaregio
show_title: On the Thousand and Second Night
curator_affiliation: Independent curator (Romanian/Moldovan)
commissioning_body: Moldova Ministry of Culture; Commissioner H.E. Oleg Nica, Ambassador to Italy
selection_method_notes: State-led; no open call documented.
reception_summary: Drone-suspended carpets reframing surveillance technology as protective. Historic first Moldovan pavilion.
red_flags: Drones as artistic medium given regional security context.

artist_name: Nomin Bold; Gerelkhuu Ganbold; Tuguldur Yondonjamts; Dorjderem Davaa
curator_affiliation: Tsultem: Indiana University; Eller: independent (former CAFA Beijing)
commissioning_body: Baigali Ochkhuu (Commissioner; founder/president of International Women's Federation of Commerce and Industry Mongolia)
selection_method_notes: No open call; continuity of curatorial team.
reception_summary: Buttafuoko (Biennale president) was decorated with Mongolia's Friendship Medal during preparations.
red_flags: Commissioner is a business-association president, not a cultural-sector figure.
Friendship Medal to Biennale president raises soft-influence questions.

show_title: Out of the Blue, I'm Swept Away
curator_affiliation: Independent curator; commissioner Vladislav Šćepanović
commissioning_body: Museum of Contemporary Art of Montenegro (MSUCG)
selection_method_notes: Organised by MSUCG.
reception_summary: Multimedia installation around Sloterdijk's 'anthropogenic greenhouses'.

artist_name: Kauw Tsitsi (Nauru) plus 9 international artists incl. CPS, Patricia Jacomella Bonola, Sylvia Grace Borda, Khaled Hafez, Stefano Cagol
show_title: AIM Inundated, Imagining Life After Land
curator_affiliation: Founder, Chamber of Public Secrets (CPS); Danish-Lebanese curator
commissioning_body: Ministry of National Heritage, Culture and Tourism of Nauru (Commissioner: Isabella Dageago, MP)
selection_method_notes: Pavilion appears to have been initiated by external curator Ramadan/CPS and accepted by Nauruan government.
public_funding_sources: Ministry of National Heritage, Culture and Tourism of Nauru
reception_summary: Strong launch press as 'historic first'. Climate-change framing (sea-level rise, phosphate extraction).
red_flags: Classic 'curator-imported' first-time pavilion: only 1 of ~10 artists is from the host country.
Associate curator Stefano Cagol is also exhibiting.

pavilion_name: New Zealand at Venice / Aotearoa NZ Pavilion
venue_label: Istituto Provinciale per l'Infanzia Santa Maria della Pietà, Castello (since 2017)
pavilion_owner: Istituto Provinciale per l'Infanzia (Veneto regional institution; leased)
artist_name: Fiona Pardington (Kāi Tahu, Kāti Mamoe, Ngāti Kahungunu)
curator_affiliation: Senior curators at Christchurch Art Gallery Te Puna o Waiwhetū
commissioning_body: Creative New Zealand / Toi Aotearoa (Commissioner: Kent Gardner)
selection_method_notes: Creative NZ ran tender for delivery partner; Christchurch Art Gallery's curators nominated Pardington.
public_funding_sources: Christchurch City Council via Christchurch Art Gallery
reception_summary: Photographic portraits of taxidermy native birds — elegiac and museologically charged.
red_flags: NZ debate about whether the ~NZ$2m-cycle Venice spend is the best use of arts funding given Creative NZ's domestic cuts.

pavilion_name: Pavilion of the Republic of North Macedonia
pavilion_owner: Patriarchate of Venice (former Catholic religious institution)
show_title: Pietà Under the Covers of Urgency
curator_affiliation: Topuzovski: Director, MoCA Skopje; Boetzkes: U. of Guelph (Canada)
commissioning_body: Ministry of Culture of North Macedonia (commissioner: Avni Qahili)
selection_method_notes: Ministry of Culture call.
public_funding_sources: Ministry of Culture of North Macedonia
reception_summary: Riffs on Michelangelo's Pietà via emergency blankets to address vulnerability and queer politics.
red_flags: Commissioner Avni Qahili is a political appointee.

venue_label: Ex Farmacia Solveni, Dorsoduro Museum Mile
artist_gallery: Grosvenor Gallery (London); Vadehra Art Gallery (Delhi); Aicon Art (NY)
curator_affiliation: Independent curator (South Asian contemporary art)
commissioning_body: Ministry of National Heritage and Culture, Pakistan
selection_method_notes: Second-ever Pakistan pavilion (after 2019). PM Shehbaz Sharif personally met the team pre-departure.
public_funding_sources: Ministry of National Heritage and Culture, Government of Pakistan
reception_summary: Significant Pakistani press; Designboom, Artsy, ArtAsiaPacific covered positively.
red_flags: Off-site location and reliance on opaque private donor base; Butt's own remark about expense suggests precarious funding.

pavilion_owner: Private historic Grand Canal warehouse, leased
artist_gallery: Cristina Guerra Contemporary Art (Lisbon); Travesía Cuatro (Madrid)
curator_affiliation: Nicolau: Director of Serralves Museum's contemporary programme
commissioning_body: Direção-Geral das Artes (DGArtes), Portuguese Ministry of Culture
selection_method_notes: Three teams invited; jury convened by DGArtes. Commissioner Américo Rodrigues.
public_funding_sources: DGArtes / Portuguese Ministry of Culture (€425,000 disclosed envelope ≈ $460K)
reception_summary: Estrela altered RedSkyFalls and joined Biennale-wide protest at the pavilion's opening over Russia and Israel inclusion.
red_flags: State-pavilion / artist-position friction with DGArtes after Estrela's protest.

pavilion_name: Pavilion of the Republic of San Marino
venue_label: Tana Art Space, Castello (between Arsenale and Giardini)
artist_gallery: Kerlin Gallery (Dublin); Maureen Paley (London); Galería Pelaires (Palma)
curator_affiliation: Independent curator/gallerist (Luca Tommasi Arte Contemporanea, Milan)
commissioning_body: FR Istituto d'Arte Contemporanea S.p.A. under State Secretariat for Education and Culture of San Marino
selection_method_notes: Direct appointment by the operating organisation, with state oversight.
public_funding_sources: State Secretariat for Education and Culture of San Marino
reception_summary: Italian-language coverage treated the pavilion as a credible abstract-painting and sound proposition.
red_flags: Recurring San Marino practice of representing the republic via foreign artists.
Private commercial-gallery curator is a mild conflict-of-interest signal.

show_title: WURUS – What the Earth provides us with
curator_affiliation: Senegalese art critic and curator (Dakar/Dak'Art scene)
commissioning_body: Ministry of Culture, Crafts and Tourism of Senegal (commissioner Oumar Sall)
selection_method_notes: Ministry-announced; no open call.
public_funding_sources: Ministry of Culture, Crafts and Tourism of Senegal
reception_summary: Astrophysicist-artist hybrid; brass/bronze/mirror installation on perception and value (gold = wurus).

pavilion_name: Pavilion of the Republic of Sierra Leone (debut)
pavilion_owner: Republic of Sierra Leone (Office of the First Lady)
artist_name: Hawa-Jane Bangura, Ayesha Feisal, Hickmatu Bintu Leigh, Abu Bakarr Mansaray + 11 international
curator_name: Sandro Orlandi Stagl and Willy Montini
curator_affiliation: Italian curators (Orlandi Stagl: long-standing Venice Biennale collateral curator)
commissioning_body: H.E. Fatima Maada Bio, First Lady of Sierra Leone
selection_method_notes: First Lady acts as commissioner; Italian curators assembled mixed roster.
public_funding_sources: Office of the First Lady of Sierra Leone
reception_summary: ART AFRICA praised it as a sensory, processual debut emphasising change and environmental justice.
red_flags: Italian curators (Orlandi Stagl is a frequent commercial-pavilion impresario) plus Italian-heavy artist line-up echoes the 'rented pavilion' pattern.
Use of First Lady's office as commissioning body is unusual and politically loaded.

artist_name: Ayan Farah, Asmaa Jama, Warsan Shire
artist_born: Farah: 1978, UAE (Somali, Sweden-raised); Jama: Mogadishu; Shire: 1988, Kenya
artist_gallery: Farah: Galerie Nordenhake; Galerie Kadel Willborn
curator_name: Mohamed Mire (Somali, Stockholm-based) and Fabio Scrivanti (Italian)
curator_affiliation: Mire: independent; Scrivanti: Italian, former project manager
commissioning_body: Office of the PM of Somalia (Cultural Advisor Abdirahman Yusuf Mohamud)
selection_method_notes: Commissioner-curator-led; advisory board reportedly all male.
public_funding_sources: Ministry of Information, Culture and Tourism
reception_summary: Public letter from queer Somali collective Warbixinta Cidda criticising: all-diaspora artist line-up, Italian co-curator, all-male advisory board.
red_flags: Italian co-curator from former colonial power.
All-male advisory board.
Reports of intimidation against Somalia-based critics of the pavilion.

artist_based: Damascus (returned permanently late 2024 after 8 years in London)
curator_affiliation: Research Professor, Kyoto University; Professor Emerita, Tokyo University of the Arts
commissioning_body: Syrian Ministry of Culture (commissioner: Waseem Abd Alhameed)
selection_method_notes: First post-Assad-era pavilion; transitional Ministry of Culture.

private_funders.name: Sara Shamma personal funding
private_funders.notes: Per The National interview, Shamma personally funded most studio production.

reception_summary: Major political-cultural event marking Syria's post-war re-engagement.
red_flags: New transitional Syrian government has limited cultural-policy infrastructure.
Pavilion produced on compressed timeline (~5 months).
Political risk: legitimising new Syrian state via cultural soft power.

venue_label: Gervasuti Foundation – Palazzo Canova / Supernova, Cannaregio
artist_name: Turakella Editha Gyindo, Lazaro Samuel, Valerie Asiimwe Amani, Amani Abeid + 13 Italian invitees
show_title: Minor Frequencies: The Inner Life of a Nation
curator_name: Lorna Benedict Mashiba and Martina Cavallarin
curator_affiliation: Mashiba: independent Tanzanian; Cavallarin: independent Italian
commissioning_body: Ministry of Information, Culture, Arts and Sports of Tanzania (Commissioner: Leah Elias Kihimbi)
selection_method_notes: No public open call; curator-led under ministry commission with significant Italian co-curatorial input.
public_funding_sources: Tanzania Ministry of Information, Culture, Arts and Sports
reception_summary: Italian-language announcement press; not picked up by major lists.
red_flags: ~Two-thirds of exhibitors are Italian or non-Tanzanian.
Gervasuti Foundation/Supernova is a known 'pay-to-play' venue platform.

artist_name: John Beadle (1964–2024) and Lavar Munroe (b. 1982) — posthumous collaboration
artist_gallery: Munroe: Larkin Durey (London); Jenkins Johnson Gallery (NY/SF)
show_title: In Another Man's Yard: John Beadle, Lavar Munroe, and the Spirit of (Posthumous) Collaboration
curator_affiliation: Mary Jane Crowe Professor, Northwestern University
commissioning_body: John Cox (Commissioner; Executive Director of Arts and Culture, Baha Mar) with Amanda Coulson (TERN Gallery, Nassau)
selection_method_notes: No open call; Cox/Coulson/Thompson assembled the artists. NAGB confirmed national participation.
public_funding_sources: No material government cash contribution disclosed; Bahamas government funding withdrawn

private_funders.name: Baha Mar (Cable Beach mega-resort)
private_funders.notes: Lead sponsor; commissioner Cox is its Executive Director of Arts and Culture.

reception_summary: ArtNews 'Biennale Standout'; Artsy '10 Best Pavilions'. Junkanoo aesthetic; first Bahamas pavilion since 2013.
red_flags: Government funding withdrawn and replaced by a single dominant corporate sponsor (Baha Mar), whose own arts executive is the pavilion commissioner — clear concentration of patronage and governance in one private entity.

artist_name: Stacey Gillian Abe, Joseph Ntensibe, Lilian Mary Nabulime, Ronex Ahimbisibwe, Lakwena Maciver, Sheila Nakitende, Aloka Trevor
curator_affiliation: Independent Ugandan painter/curator
commissioning_body: Government of Uganda; Commissioner Acaye Kerunen (Uganda's 2022 representative artist)
selection_method_notes: Kerunen appointed Commissioner by the Government; she invited curator and artists.
reception_summary: Pre-opening coverage in Contemporary And, Africanah, ART AFRICA.
red_flags: Repeats 2022 pattern where Kerunen, herself an artist who represented Uganda last edition, now controls commissioner role.

artist_name: Group of 13 lacquer artists incl. Le Huu Hieu
show_title: Viet Nam: Art in the Global Flow
curator_affiliation: Independent (formerly Asia Art Archive; PhD candidate Cornell)
commissioning_body: Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism (commissioner: Mã Thế Anh)
selection_method_notes: No public open call. First-ever official Vietnamese national pavilion.
public_funding_sources: Vietnam Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism
reception_summary: Historic-debut framing; lacquer focus considered safe and traditional.
red_flags: State-curated artist roster skews toward traditional lacquer establishment.
No critical or diaspora Vietnamese voices included.

venue_label: Santa Maria della Pietà / Chiesa della Visitazione, Castello
artist_name: Gideon Gomo, Eva Raath, Felix Shumba, Franklyn Dzingai, Pardon Mapondera
curator_affiliation: Curator at the National Gallery of Zimbabwe
commissioning_body: National Gallery of Zimbabwe (Commissioner: Raphael Chikukwa, Executive Director)
selection_method_notes: Selection internal to National Gallery of Zimbabwe under Chikukwa's commissionership.
public_funding_sources: Government of Zimbabwe (Ministry of Sport, Recreation, Arts and Culture)
reception_summary: Chikukwa has now commissioned every Zimbabwe pavilion since 2011.
red_flags: Chikukwa has now served as commissioner for ~7 consecutive editions — concentration concerns.
